steps.title: PDL solution 5 mg of Poly-D-lysine...
steps.instruction: PDL solution 5 mg of Poly-D-lysine hydrobromide in 50 mL of ultrapure water.

steps.title: Culturing medium Reagent Final concentration Quantity...
steps.instruction: Culturing medium Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume B-27 plus supplement 50× 1× 1 mL GlutaMAX 100× 1× 500 μL Gentamicin 5 μg/mL 5 μL Neurobasal Plus n/a 48.5 mL Total n/a 50 mL Prepare and equilibrate in a CO 2 incubator one day prior to dissection.

steps.title: 4% PFA solution 1 mL of...
steps.instruction: 4% PFA solution 1 mL of 16% PFA in 3 mL of PBS, pH 7.

steps.title: Glyoxal buffer Reagent Final concentration Quantity...
steps.instruction: Glyoxal buffer Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume Ethyl alcohol, pure 21.6% 39.45 mL Acetic acid glacial 0.82% 1.5 mL Ultrapure water n/a 141.75 mL 1 M NaOH n/a Titrate to pH 5 Total n/a ~200 mL Add 0.856 mL of 40 wt. % glyoxal solution to 10 mL of glyoxal buffer prior to fixation [11].

steps.title: Blocking and permeabilization buffer Reagent Final...
steps.instruction: Blocking and permeabilization buffer Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume PBS (1×) 50% 5 mL Fish serum 50% 5 mL Triton X-100 0.5% 50 μL Total n/a 10 mL For surface labeling with antibody, block without adding Triton X-100 and permeabilize after surface labeling. Blocking is primarily for antibody co-staining. When using Sylites alone, serum (either fish serum or 3% BSA) can be omitted without introducing nonspecific labeling. Laboratory supplies

steps.title: Freer elevator/spatula
steps.instruction: Freer elevator/spatula

steps.title: 60 mm Petri dish (Celltreat, catalog number:
steps.instruction: 60 mm Petri dish (Celltreat, catalog number:

steps.title: Ultra-fine forceps (Excelta Corp, model
steps.instruction: Ultra-fine forceps (Excelta Corp, model: 7-SA)

steps.title: 150 mm Petri dish (Celltreat Scientific, catalog number
steps.instruction: 150 mm Petri dish (Celltreat Scientific, catalog number: 229656)

steps.title: One day prior to P0–P1 mouse...
steps.instruction: One day prior to P0–P1 mouse dissection, prepare coated coverslips by putting 18 mm #1.5 coverslips in individual wells of a 12-well plate.

steps.title: Add 270 μL of PDL solution...
steps.instruction: Add 270 μL of PDL solution on top of the coverslips. Ensure that the solution forms a stable meniscus on top of the coverslip without spilling over to the bottom of the well, as shown in Figure 1 . Figure

steps.title: Poly-D-lysine (PDL) coating on coverslips
steps.instruction: Poly-D-lysine (PDL) coating on coverslips. Limiting PDL to the coverslip surface restricts cells to the coverslip during seeding, preventing them from spreading to the bottom of the plate.

steps.title: Incubate the 12-well plate overnight at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate the 12-well plate overnight at 4 °C.

steps.title: Prepare culturing medium in a 50...
steps.instruction: Prepare culturing medium in a 50 mL T25 flask with a vented cap or in a 50 mL conical tube with the cap loosely closed. Incubate in a CO 2 incubator overnight at 37 °C.

steps.title: Before dissection, rinse the coverslips three...
steps.instruction: Before dissection, rinse the coverslips three times with ultrapure water and allow them to air-dry inside the biosafety cabinet with the lid removed. Air drying usually takes approximately 1 h and is normally completed by the time the dissection steps are finished.

steps.title: Decapitate E18 or P0–P1 pups at...
steps.instruction: Decapitate E18 or P0–P1 pups at the base of the skull, then stabilize the head by inserting a pair of thin straight forceps into the eye sockets ( Video 1 ).

steps.title: Cut the skin along the sagittal...
steps.instruction: Cut the skin along the sagittal suture from the posterior to the anterior side with 3.5 mm Castroviejo micro scissors.

steps.title: Cut the skull along the sagittal...
steps.instruction: Cut the skull along the sagittal suture from the posterior to the anterior side and along the bone plate below the ears on both sides.

steps.title: Carefully scoop out the brain by...
steps.instruction: Carefully scoop out the brain by using a freer elevator or a spatula and immerse it in precooled HBSS solution without calcium and magnesium in a 60 mm Petri dish.

steps.title: Slice off the cortex from the...
steps.instruction: Slice off the cortex from the midbrain by using a scalpel. Video

steps.title: Isolation of cerebral hemispheres from P0–P1 mouse neonates
steps.instruction: Isolation of cerebral hemispheres from P0–P1 mouse neonates

steps.title: Using a dissection microscope, carefully peel...
steps.instruction: Using a dissection microscope, carefully peel off the meninges with ultrafine forceps ( Video 2 ).

steps.title: Orient the hemisphere with the dorsal...
steps.instruction: Orient the hemisphere with the dorsal side of the brain facing upward. The hippocampi should be clearly visible.

steps.title: Remove the hippocampi from the cortex by using forceps
steps.instruction: Remove the hippocampi from the cortex by using forceps. For detailed procedures and pictures, see [12]. Video

steps.title: Isolation of hippocampi from cerebral hemispheres
steps.instruction: Isolation of hippocampi from cerebral hemispheres

steps.title: Transfer the isolated hippocampi into a...
steps.instruction: Transfer the isolated hippocampi into a 5 mL conical tube containing fresh HBSS without Ca 2+ and Mg 2+ solution and keep on ice while dissecting the remaining animals (two pups are sufficient to prepare one 12-well plate).

steps.title: Remove the HBSS solution and rinse with prewarmed 0
steps.instruction: Remove the HBSS solution and rinse with prewarmed 0.05% trypsin-EDTA solution twice.

steps.title: Incubate the tissue in 5 mL of fresh, prewarmed 0
steps.instruction: Incubate the tissue in 5 mL of fresh, prewarmed 0.05% trypsin-EDTA solution at 37 °C for 15 min, gently flipping the tube midway through the incubation.

steps.title: Remove the trypsin-EDTA solution, then quench...
steps.instruction: Remove the trypsin-EDTA solution, then quench any residual enzymatic activity by washing the tissue three times with HBSS containing calcium and magnesium.

steps.title: Replace the solution with 1 mL of pre-equilibrated culture medium
steps.instruction: Replace the solution with 1 mL of pre-equilibrated culture medium.

steps.title: Carefully triturate the tissue using a...
steps.instruction: Carefully triturate the tissue using a 1 mL pipette tip trimmed to different pore sizes: 11 times with a large-pore tip, 11 times with a medium-pore tip, and 11 times with an uncut tip.

steps.title: Allow the undissociated tissue chunks to...
steps.instruction: Allow the undissociated tissue chunks to settle by gravity for 1 min.

steps.title: Take out the supernatant and perform...
steps.instruction: Take out the supernatant and perform a cell count using a hemocytometer with a 5–10-fold dilution.

steps.title: Dilute cell suspension to a final...
steps.instruction: Dilute cell suspension to a final density of 360,000 cells/mL with pre-equilibrated culture medium.

steps.title: Add 250 μL of the suspended...
steps.instruction: Add 250 μL of the suspended cells on top of the pre-coated coverslip. The solution should form a stable meniscus on top of the coverslip without spilling over to the bottom of the well.

steps.title: Incubate the cells in a CO...
steps.instruction: Incubate the cells in a CO 2 incubator for at least 2 h for the cells to adhere onto the coverslip.

steps.title: Add 750 μL of culture medium...
steps.instruction: Add 750 μL of culture medium to make up the volume to 1 mL.

steps.title: Incubate the cell in a CO...
steps.instruction: Incubate the cell in a CO 2 incubator for at least 14 days and refresh 40% of the culture medium every 7 days. B. Neuron fixation

steps.title: Fix the neurons at the desired...
steps.instruction: Fix the neurons at the desired days in vitro (DIV) by first removing the culture medium and rinsing twice with PBS, followed by a 15-min incubation with either 4% PFA or glyoxal solution. Ensure that cells do not dry out during solution changes. While glyoxal fixation may offer improved image quality, no significant difference in labeling efficiency has been observed between PFA and glyoxal.

steps.title: Remove fixation solution and gently wash...
steps.instruction: Remove fixation solution and gently wash the cells with PBS for 5 min on an orbital shaker, repeating the wash three times. Pause point: Fixed neurons can be stored at 4 °C after fixation for up to 2 weeks. Critical: Sylite labeling is sensitive toward the choice of fixation reagent. eSylite labeling is sensitive to the integrity of the PDZ binding pockets. Cells stored for more than one month may exhibit collapsed binding pockets, resulting in a loss of eSylite signal, while PSD-95 antibody staining may still be detectable. Caution: Glyoxal and PFA are environmentally hazardous. Quench excess aldehydes by incubating with 1 M glycine for 15 min before discarding the solution down the drain. C. Sylite labeling

steps.title: Block and permeabilize the cells using...
steps.instruction: Block and permeabilize the cells using a blocking/permeabilization solution (see Recipe

steps.title: for at least 30 min at room temperature
steps.instruction: for at least 30 min at room temperature. Cells can be kept in the blocking/permeabilization solution for 1–2 days at 4 °C. Note: For extracellular labeling, perform blocking and permeabilization separately, omitting Triton X-100 during the blocking step. Fish serum or 3% BSA in PBS can be used as the blocking reagent. Blocking is not necessary when using Sylites alone and is primarily required when additional antibodies are included in the labeling cocktail.

steps.title: Dilute the Sylite solution to a...
steps.instruction: Dilute the Sylite solution to a final concentration of 25–100 nM in blocking and permeabilization solution. While labeling is possible at concentrations as low as 1 nM, lower concentrations may result in incomplete labeling and reduced signal intensity. Additional antibodies can be included in the same solution for multichannel imaging.

steps.title: Add 100μL of the Sylite-containing blocking...
steps.instruction: Add 100μL of the Sylite-containing blocking and permeabilization solution onto a flat sheet of Parafilm placed in a 150 mm Petri dish.

steps.title: Carefully pick up the coverslips with...
steps.instruction: Carefully pick up the coverslips with forceps, flip them, and place them cell-side down onto the Sylite solution for incubation. Incubate for at least 30 min at room temperature or overnight at 4°C. Incubation for 30 min or 2 h at room temperature did not show a discernible difference under diffraction-limited imaging; however, 1 h at room temperature is recommended to ensure efficient co-labeling with antibodies in the cocktail. Avoid exposing the samples to strong light after Sylite labeling. For overnight incubation, place a wet Kimwipe inside the Petri dish to maintain humidity and prevent the solution from drying out.

steps.title: Transfer and flip the coverslip back to a 12-well plate
steps.instruction: Transfer and flip the coverslip back to a 12-well plate. Gently wash the cells with PBS for 5 min on an orbital shaker; repeat the wash three times.

steps.title: Secondary antibody labeling can be performed...
steps.instruction: Secondary antibody labeling can be performed at this step, if needed. D. Imaging with fluorescence microscope

steps.title: For confocal/STED microscopy, add one drop...
steps.instruction: For confocal/STED microscopy, add one drop of ProLong TM Gold antifade mountant onto a clean microscope slide. Pick up the coverslip and gently blot the edge with a Kimwipe to remove excess PBS. Carefully place the coverslip onto the mountant, cell-side down, and avoid trapping air bubbles during mounting. Critical: Sylite with sulfoCy5 labeled, designed for dSTORM, is not photostable enough even with the mountant. Imaging with the sulfoCy5 version of Sylite should be done with less laser power and multiple line average and frame accumulation to increase the signal-to-noise (S/N) ratio and signal intensity. The STAR635P version of Sylite is photostable and easier to image with confocal and STED microscopy.

steps.title: Allow the mountant to cure at...
steps.instruction: Allow the mountant to cure at room temperature for 24 h, keeping the slide protected from light during this period.

steps.title: Rinse the coverslip surface with ultrapure...
steps.instruction: Rinse the coverslip surface with ultrapure water and gently blot dry to remove any residual PBS salts, which can damage the objective lens. Pause point: Mounted coverslips can be stored at 4°C in the dark for several weeks without significant signal loss.

steps.title: Proceed with imaging according to the...
steps.instruction: Proceed with imaging according to the specific instructions for your microscope system.

description: Validation of protocol Images of Sylites applications in fixed primary neuron imaging are shown in Figure 2 . Figure 2. Image gallery of Sylite probes. (A) Confocal imaging of eSylite (sCy5-labeled) in 23 days in vitro (DIV) dissociated neuronal cultures, co-labeled with antibodies against MAP2, GluA2, and GluN2B. (B) tau-STED imaging of STAR635P-labeled eSylite in eGFP-transfected dissociated 21 DIV cortical neurons, co-labeled with a PSD-95 antibody visualized by Alexa Fluor 594-conjugated secondary antibody. Cumulative frequency distributions of FWHM of tau-STED-resolved nanoclusters labeled by the antibody (cyan line, n = 236 nanoclusters) and eSylite (magenta line, n = 239 nanoclusters; ***p < 0.0001, K−S test). Reprinted (adapted) with permission from [1]. (C) Confocal imaging of iSylite (sCy5-labeled) in 20 DIV dissociated neuronal cultures, co-labeled with antibodies against VGAT and VGlut1. This protocol or parts of it has been used and validated in the following research article: Huhn et al. [1]. eSylites: Synthetic Probes for Visualization and Topographic Mapping of Single Excitatory Synapses. Journal of the American Chemical Society . Khayenko et al. [2]. A Versatile Synthetic Affinity Probe Reveals Inhibitory Synapse Ultrastructure and Brain Connectivity. Angewandte Chemie International Edition .
